summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is a glide reflection?

A reflection flips a figure over a line (axis of reflection). The image is the same distance from the line as the original, on the opposite side. The answer is A translation followed by a reflection over a line parallel to the translation vector. The other options — 'Two reflections over the same line', 'A rotation followed by a dilation', and 'A dilation followed by a translation' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the result of composing two isometries?

Properties The composition of two isometries is always an isometry. The other options — 'Always a dilation', 'Sometimes an isometry, sometimes not', and 'Never an isometry' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
